scope: Analytics event storage module (7 files across core/analytics, hooks, CLI, shell scripts)

validation.note: Standalone review - findings verified against source code by orchestrator

findings.title: Singleton ignores projectRoot after first instantiation
findings.description: getInstance(projectRoot) only uses projectRoot on first call. Subsequent calls with a different root silently return the collector bound to the original root. The CLI command at analytics.ts:122 calls getInstance(projectRoot) but this is a no-op if the singleton exists from an earlier call.
findings.impact: Cross-project data corruption. Events written to wrong project directory.
findings.recommendation: Validate requested root matches existing instance and throw/reset if not, or use per-root factory cache.

findings.title: TOCTOU race condition in log rotation causes data loss
findings.description: checkRotation reads file size, then rotateLog reads entire file, filters, and rewrites. Events appended between readFileSync and writeFileSync by concurrent writers (shell script, hook handler) are silently dropped.
findings.impact: Permanent data loss of analytics events during rotation.
findings.recommendation: Use atomic rename (write to temp, rename over original) or file locking.

findings.title: Shell script JSON injection via unescaped variables
findings.description: EVENT_NAME, EVENT_TYPE, PLUGIN, and INCREMENT are interpolated into JSON without escaping. Only ERROR has partial escaping. A name containing double quotes breaks JSON structure.
findings.impact: Malformed JSON lines in events.jsonl. Downstream readers silently skip them, causing undercounting. Could inject arbitrary JSON fields.
findings.recommendation: Use jq for JSON construction when available, or escape all string values like ERROR is escaped.

findings.title: readEventsFiltered until-filter excludes all events on end date
findings.description: String comparison event.timestamp > options.until fails when until is date-only (e.g., '2026-03-30'). All events on that date have timestamps like '2026-03-30T14:00:00Z' which are lexicographically greater, so they get excluded.
findings.impact: Users querying --until 2026-03-30 see no events from March 30th.
findings.recommendation: Normalize date-only strings by appending T23:59:59.999Z before comparison.

findings.title: Hook handler appendEvent uses Record<string, unknown> bypassing type safety
findings.description: appendEvent accepts Record<string, unknown> instead of AnalyticsEvent. The compiler cannot enforce that events have required fields. Additionally, the hook bypasses AnalyticsCollector entirely, meaning no rotation check occurs.
findings.impact: Required fields can be omitted without compile-time error. Events file can grow beyond maxLogSizeBytes without rotation.
findings.recommendation: Type parameter as AnalyticsEvent. Consider using AnalyticsCollector.getInstance() instead of direct file append.

findings.title: Unsafe as AnalyticsEvent cast on JSON.parse without validation
findings.description: JSON.parse(line) as AnalyticsEvent at lines 207 and 249 asserts shape without runtime validation. Malformed events (from hook handler or corrupted file) silently produce structurally invalid objects.
findings.impact: Downstream code accessing .success, .type, .name gets undefined while TS believes they are defined. Incorrect aggregation results.
findings.recommendation: Use a type guard isAnalyticsEvent(parsed): parsed is AnalyticsEvent that checks required fields.

findings.title: ensureInitialized permanently disables analytics on transient error
findings.description: If mkdirSync throws (e.g., transient permission error, disk full then freed), the catch sets this.config.enabled = false permanently. No retry mechanism exists. All subsequent track() calls are silently no-ops for the rest of the process.
findings.impact: A single transient filesystem error permanently disables analytics collection for the entire CLI session.
findings.recommendation: Reset initialized flag to allow retry on next track() call, or add a retry counter.

findings.title: getSummaryWithCache ignores limit in filter detection
findings.description: hasFilters checks since, until, type, plugin but not limit. A call with { limit: 5 } returns a cached summary built with limit 10, silently returning wrong top-N lists.
findings.impact: Users get wrong number of items in top lists when using non-default limits after cache is populated.
findings.recommendation: Include limit in hasFilters check, or make limit part of the cache key.

findings.title: AnalyticsCommandResult silently drops UsageCount fields
findings.description: topCommands/topSkills/topAgents are typed as Array<{ name, count }> but the actual data is UsageCount[] with successCount, failureCount, avgDuration, plugin, lastUsed. Consumers cannot access richer data without unsafe widening.
findings.impact: API consumers lose access to success/failure counts, duration, plugin info.
findings.recommendation: Change type to UsageCount[].

findings.title: Log rotation completely untested
findings.description: No test triggers checkRotation or validates rotateLog. Archive directory creation, file naming, line filtering, and the TOCTOU race are all unexercised.
findings.impact: Silent data loss or corruption during rotation goes undetected.
findings.recommendation: Test with maxLogSizeBytes=100, verify rotation archives old events and preserves recent ones.

findings.title: Shell $EVENTS variable grows unbounded in memory
findings.description: Entire filtered event set accumulated in a shell variable via string concatenation. For large files (up to 10MB), multiple grep passes re-parse the full string each time.
findings.impact: High memory usage and O(n^2) performance for large datasets.
findings.recommendation: Use awk or jq for single-pass streaming processing.

findings.title: No input validation on event names in TypeScript collector
findings.description: name parameter in trackCommand/trackSkill/trackAgent has no length limit or control character filtering. Extremely long names could bloat the events file.
findings.impact: File bloat from excessively long event names. Control characters cause display issues.
findings.recommendation: Reject names over 256 chars and strip control characters.

findings.title: Unsafe as AnalyticsSummary cast on cache file
findings.description: JSON.parse(content) as AnalyticsSummary casts unvalidated cache content. Older cache versions with different schema produce invalid objects.
findings.impact: Runtime errors in displaySummary when cache has missing fields.
findings.recommendation: Validate key fields before returning, or version the cache format.

findings.title: Archive file naming collision on same-day rotation
findings.description: Archive filename uses date-only (events-2026-03-30.jsonl). Multiple rotations on same day append to same archive file, producing overlapping date ranges.
findings.impact: Confusing archive semantics. Data preserved via append but analysis is unreliable.
findings.recommendation: Include timestamp or sequence number in filename.

findings.title: Unbounded file growth from hook handler path
findings.description: Hook handler writes events directly to disk, bypassing AnalyticsCollector. Since checkRotation only runs in the collector's track() method, events from hooks never trigger rotation. If most writes come from hooks (~55% of events), the file can grow well beyond maxLogSizeBytes.
findings.impact: Events file grows unchecked until a collector write happens to trigger rotation.
findings.recommendation: Have hook handler call checkRotation or use the collector singleton.

findings.title: Unsafe as string casts in hook handler
findings.description: Casts unknown values directly to string without typeof check. If runtime value is a number or object, skillName.includes(':') would throw.
findings.impact: Runtime TypeError if hook input contains non-string tool_name.
findings.recommendation: Use typeof guard: const explicit = typeof input.tool_name === 'string' ? input.tool_name : ''

findings.title: Cache TTL expiry path untested
findings.description: The age > cacheTTLMs branch (stale cache) is never exercised. TTL is hardcoded with no time injection.
findings.impact: Stale cache bugs go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Test with fs.utimesSync to simulate old mtime.

findings.title: track-analytics.sh usage header omits --failure flag
findings.description: Usage header documents --success, --error, --duration but not --failure. The --failure flag was recently added but not documented.
findings.impact: Users don't know --failure exists.
findings.recommendation: Add [--failure] to usage lines.

findings.title: Legacy event normalization (success=undefined) untested
findings.description: The code defaults parsed.success = true when undefined, but no test writes a raw JSONL line without success and verifies normalization.
findings.impact: If normalization is removed, old events cause NaN in success-rate calculations.
findings.recommendation: Test with raw JSONL line missing success field.

findings.title: post-tool-use-analytics.ts module JSDoc hardcodes wrong path
findings.description: Comment says 'Appends events to .specweave/state/analytics/events.jsonl' but actual path comes from context.stateDir parameter.
findings.impact: Misleading documentation for maintainers.
findings.recommendation: Change to 'Appends events to <stateDir>/analytics/events.jsonl'.

findings.title: parseSinceFilter accepts 0 and extremely large values
findings.description: Regex accepts 0h, 0d, 99999d. 0d means 'now', showing no historical data. Large values produce pre-epoch dates.
findings.impact: Confusing but not crashing.
findings.recommendation: Reject 0 and cap at a reasonable maximum.

findings.title: Shell integer division for success rate
findings.description: Bash integer division truncates (99.7% shows as 99%). TS aggregator uses floating point with one decimal.
findings.impact: Minor display difference between shell and TS dashboards.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for shell. Document the difference or use bc.

findings.title: Shell per-command success rate counts wrong events
findings.description: Grep for name matches across all event types. If a skill and command share a name, success rate includes wrong type's events.
findings.impact: Incorrect per-item success rate when names collide across types.
findings.recommendation: Add type filter to grep.

findings.title: CSV export without jq produces empty file
findings.description: CSV export only writes data rows when jq is available. Without jq, file has header only with no warning.
findings.impact: User gets empty CSV with no indication jq is required.
findings.recommendation: Add else branch with warning or grep fallback.

findings.title: AnalyticsConfig not readonly
findings.description: Config fields are mutable. DEFAULT_ANALYTICS_CONFIG properties can be mutated. The collector spreads at construction so mutation of the default won't propagate, but the interface allows accidental mutation.
findings.impact: Potential accidental mutation of unrelated config fields.
findings.recommendation: Use Readonly<AnalyticsConfig> for the config property.

findings.title: parseSinceFilter switch has no default/exhaustiveness check
findings.description: unit is typed as string (from regex). Adding a new unit letter compiles without error but silently returns wrong date.
findings.impact: Future maintenance risk.
findings.recommendation: Type unit as 'h'|'d'|'w'|'m' and add default: unit satisfies never.

findings.title: Error messages may contain sensitive context
findings.description: The error field stores error messages that could contain file paths, env var names, or partial config details. Persisted in plaintext.
findings.impact: Low for local CLI. Could leak details in shared repos via analytics exports.
findings.recommendation: Truncate error messages and strip sensitive patterns.

findings.title: File permissions are process-inherited (world-readable)
findings.description: Neither TS nor shell explicitly set file permissions. Files inherit process umask (typically 0644, world-readable).
findings.impact: On shared systems, other users could read analytics data.
findings.recommendation: Set directory permissions to 0700 and file permissions to 0600.

testGaps.title: Log rotation never triggered or validated
testGaps.suggestedTest: Given collector with maxLogSizeBytes=100 / When enough events written to exceed limit / Then events.jsonl has only recent events and archive/ contains old events

testGaps.title: readEventsFiltered date-range (since/until) never tested
testGaps.suggestedTest: Given events spanning 3 days / When readEventsFiltered({ since: middleDay, until: endDay }) / Then only events within range returned

testGaps.title: Cache TTL expiry path untested
testGaps.suggestedTest: Given cache file with mtime > 5min / When getSummaryWithCache() / Then recomputes and returns fresh summary

testGaps.title: Legacy event normalization untested
testGaps.suggestedTest: Given JSONL line without success field / When readEvents() / Then returned event has success=true

testGaps.title: Malformed JSONL line skipping during rotation untested
testGaps.suggestedTest: Given mix of valid and malformed JSONL lines / When rotation triggers / Then malformed lines dropped, valid lines preserved
